summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements across frontend and backend. Key features like product catalog, cart/checkout with Stripe, wishlist, rewards, chatbot, customer care, reviews, and authentication are all present. However, several specific acceptance criteria have gaps: the Login and Register pages do not show email+password form flows (auth uses Internet Identity only), animated candle backgrounds on auth pages are unclear, pagination on product listing is not evidenced, and the purchase-gate on review submission needs verification.

missing_requirements.requirement: Login page supporting sign-in via email+password credentials. Registration page accepting name, email, phone number, password. Both pages display animated candle visual elements in the background.
missing_requirements.reason: The auth system is Internet Identity-based (useInternetIdentity hook). Login and Register pages exist as routes but the diff does not show email+password form fields or animated candle backgrounds on those pages. The ProfileSetupModal collects name/email/phone after II login but is not a registration page with password support.

missing_requirements.requirement: Public listing query returns paginated product results.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend getProducts() is called without pagination parameters in useGetProducts hook. No evidence of pagination (page number, page size, or cursor) in the visible backend or frontend query code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Points are awarded to user account after a completed order.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo is truncated and the automatic point-awarding logic tied to order completion is not confirmed in the visible code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Only users with a completed purchase of the product can submit a review.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo is truncated and the purchase-gate validation for review submission is not confirmed in the visible portion of the code.

hallucinated_features.description: PaymentSetup admin configuration modal for entering Stripe secret keys at runtime
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies using Stripe as the payment gateway but does not request an admin UI to configure Stripe secret keys dynamically. The PaymentSetup.tsx component allows admins to enter and save a Stripe secret key via a dialog, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Post-login ProfileSetupModal replacing a dedicated Registration page
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec requires a separate Registration page with email, phone, password, and Google OAuth fields. The implementation uses a modal dialog triggered after Internet Identity login instead of a standalone registration page flow.
